name: Student loans (US)
summary: Federal and private student loans — major welfare category with complex rate / forgiveness / repayment trade-offs.

evidence.claim: Federal Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized loans have borrower-side protections private lenders do not: income-driven repayment (IDR), PSLF forgiveness, disability discharge, death discharge, deferment/forbearance flexibility.

Private student loans at lower advertised rates frequently require co-signers and lack federal borrower protections. Refinancing federal loans to private waives PSLF/IDR eligibility — usually permanently.

criteria.name: loan_type
criteria.notes: Federal offers protections private does not (E1, E2).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Private student loan rates are credit-based and individualized; 'lowest' is a pre-qualification-only teaser. Your actual rate may be far higher.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cited rate the teaser (credit-conditional) or actual offer?

confabulationPatterns.pattern: refinance your federal loans to save
confabulationPatterns.reality: Refinancing federal -> private permanently waives PSLF, IDR, disability/death discharge, and federal forbearance (E2). 'Savings' may be net-negative over a career.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the refinance calculator accounted for the loss of federal protections? PSLF alone can forgive $50k-$200k.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Variable rates rise in rising-rate environments; the 'lower payment' is point-in-time. Fixed rates lock the cost.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the variable rate have a cap? Is the user prepared for rate increases?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Debt-relief / loan-forgiveness service charging upfront fees
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — CFPB has prosecuted multiple debt-relief scammers; federal forgiveness is always free through studentaid.gov

compatibilityQuestions: Eligible for Public Service Loan Forgiveness (government, 501(c)(3) nonprofit)? Keep loans federal.
Private-sector high-income career? Refinancing federal-to-private MAY make sense if PSLF isn't on the table.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Origination fees (4-5% on federal PLUS; variable on private)
